conversation_history.content:
1→# Changelog
  2→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4→
  5→## Unreleased
  6→
  7→### Added
  8→
  9→### Removed
 10→
 11→### Fixed
 12→* Store path of file containing a lint violation relative to the location of the baseline file itself ([#1962](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1962))
 13→* Print absolute path of file in lint violations when flag "--relative" is not specified in Ktlint CLI ([#1963](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1963)) 
 14→* Handle parameter `--code-style=android_studio` in Ktlint CLI identical to deprecated parameter `--android` ([#1982](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1982))
 15→* Prevent nullpointer exception (NPE) if class without body is followed by multiple blank lines until end of file `no-consecutive-blank-lines` ([#1987](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1987))
 16→* Allow to 'unset' the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_function_signature_rule_force_multiline_when_parameter_count_greater_or_equal_than` when using `ktlint_official` code style `function-signature` ([#1977](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1977))
 17→* Prevent nullpointer exception (NPE) if or operator at start of line is followed by dot qualified expression `indent` ([#1993](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1993))
 18→
 19→### Changed
 20→* Separated Baseline functionality out of `ktlint-cli` into separate `ktlint-baseline` module for API consumers
 21→
 22→## [0.49.0] - 2023-04-21
 23→
 24→WARNING: This version of KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgraded before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
 25→
 26→All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
 27→* The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI.
 28→* The `.editorconfig` properties used to enable or disable rule and rule sets. Note that properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` have been removed in this release. See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.
 29→* The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
 30→* The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
 31→* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
 32→
 33→### Moving experimental rules to standard rule set
 34→
 35→The `experimental` rule set has been merged with `standard` rule set. The rules which formerly were part of the `experimental` rule set are still being treated as before. The rules will only be run in case `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or in case the rule is explicitly enabled.
 36→
 37→Note that the prefix `experimental:` has to be removed from all references to this rule. Check references in:
 38→* The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI.
 39→* The `.editorconfig` properties used to enable or disable rule and rule sets. Note that properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` have been removed in this release. See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.
 40→* The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
 41→* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
 42→
 43→### Promote experimental rules to non-experimental
 44→
 45→The rules below have been promoted to non-experimental rules:
 46→* [block-comment-initial-star-alignment](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#block-comment-initial-star-alignment)
 47→* [class-naming](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#classobject-naming)
 48→* [comment-wrapping](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#comment-wrapping)
 49→* [function-return-type-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#function-return-type-spacing)
 50→* [function-start-of-body-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#function-start-of-body-spacing)
 51→* [function-type-reference-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#function-type-reference-spacing)
 52→* [fun-keyword-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#fun-keyword-spacing)
 53→* [kdoc-wrapping](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#kdoc-wrapping)
 54→* [modifier-list-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#modifier-list-spacing)
 55→* [nullable-type-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#nullable-type-spacing)
 56→* [spacing-between-function-name-and-opening-parenthesis](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#spacing-between-function-name-and-opening-parenthesis)
 57→* [unnecessary-parentheses-before-trailing-lambda](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#unnecessary-parenthesis-before-trailing-lambda)
 58→
 59→Note that this only affects users that have enabled the standard ruleset while having the experimental rules disabled.
 60→
 61→### API Changes & RuleSet providers & Reporter Providers
 62→
 63→This release is intended to be the last release before the 1.0.x release of ktlint. If all goes as planned, the 1.0.x release does not contain any new breaking changes with except of removal of functionality which is deprecated in this release.
 64→
 65→This release contains a lot of breaking changes which aims to improve the future maintainability of Ktlint. If you get stuck while migrating, please reach out to us by creating an issue.
 66→
 67→#### Experimental rules
 68→
 69→Rules in custom rule sets can now be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint if `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or if the rule itself has been enabled explicitly.
 70→
 71→When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set as that would require a distinct rule set id. When moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set this would mean that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
 72→
 73→#### EditorConfig
 74→
 75→Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. New fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
 76→
 77→The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are no longer supported. Specifying those properties in the `editorConfigOverride` or `editorConfigDefaults` result in warnings at runtime.
 78→
 79→#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
 80→
 81→A new code style `ktlint_official` is introduced. This code style is work in progress but will become the default code style in the `1.0` release. Please try out the new code style and provide your feedback via the [issue tracker](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues).
 82→
 83→This `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This code style also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in those conventions and style guide. But do note that this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor.
 84→
 85→The existing code styles have been renamed to make more clear what the basis of the code style is.
 86→
 87→The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint `0.49`. Note that the default code style will be changed to `ktlint_official` in the `1.0` release.
 88→
 89→Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
 90→
 91→#### Package restructuring and class relocation
 92→
 93→The internal structure of the Ktlint project has been revised. The Ktlint CLI and KtLint API modules have been decoupled where possible. Modules have been restructured and renamed. See [API Overview](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/api/overview/) for more information.
 94→
 95→This is the last release that contains module `ktlint-core` as it had too many responsibilities. All classes in this module are relocated to other modules. Some classes have also been renamed. See tables below for details. Classes that are left behind in the `ktlint-core` module are deprecated and were kept in this version for backwards compatibility only. The `ktlint-core` module will be removed in Ktlint `0.50.x`.
 96→
 97→Classes below have been moved from module `ktlint-core` to the new module `ktlint-rule-engine-core`:
 98→
 99→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`                  | New class/package name in `ktlint-rule-engine-core`                               |
100→|----------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
101→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.editorconfig               | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig                            |
102→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigProperties     | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.EditorConfig                            |
103→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.OptInFeatures              | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.OptInFeatures                           |
104→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType                | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType                             |
105→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.package                    | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ASTNodeExtension                        |
106→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.IndentConfig                   | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig                            |
107→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Rule                           | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule                                    |
108→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleProvider                   | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleProvider                            |
109→
110→Classes below have been moved from module `ktlint-core` to the new module `ktlint-rule-engine`:
111→
112→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`                  | New class/package name in `ktlint-rule-engine`                               |
113→|----------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
114→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigDefaults       | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.EditorConfigDefaults                    |
115→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigOverride       | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.EditorConfigOverride                    |
116→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.KtLintParseException       | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintParseException                    |
117→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.KtLintRuleException        | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintRuleException                     |
118→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.KtLint                         | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintRuleEngine                        |
119→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.LintError                      | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.LintError                               |
120→
121→Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.KtLint.Code.CodeFile` has been replaced with factory method `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.Code.fromFile`. Likewise, class `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.KtLint.Code.CodeSnippet` has been replaced with factory method `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.Code.fromSnippet`.
122→
123→Class below has been moved from module `ktlint-core` to the new module `ktlint-cli-ruleset-core`:
124→
125→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`                  | New class/package name in `ktlint-cli-ruleset-core`                           |
126→|----------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
127→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleSetProviderV2              | com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.ruleset.core.api.RuleSetProviderV3                   |
128→
129→
130→Class below has been moved from module `ktlint-core` to the new module `ktlint-cli-reporter-core`:
131→
132→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`   | New class/package name in `ktlint-cli-reporter-core`     |
133→|-------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------|
134→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.KtlintVersion   | com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.reporter.core.api.KtlintVersion |
135→
136→Class below has been moved from module `ktlint-core` to the new module `ktlint-logger`:
137→
138→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`               | New class/package name in `ktlint-logger`                   |
139→|-------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------|
140→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.KtLintKLoggerInitializer.kt | com.pinterest.ktlint.logger.api.KtLintKLoggerInitializer.kt |
141→
142→Class below has been relocated from module `ktlint-core` to module `ktlint-cli`:
143→
144→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core` | New class/package name in `ktlint-cli` |
145→|-----------------------------------------|----------------------------------------|
146→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.Baseline  | com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.api.Baseline  |
147→
148→Module `ktlint-reporter-baseline` has been renamed to `ktlint-cli-reporter-baseline`. Class below has been relocated:
149→
150→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-reporter-baseline` | New class/package name in `ktlint-cli-reporter-baseline` |
151→|------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------|
152→| com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.baseline               | com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.reporter.baseline               |
153→
154→Module `ktlint-reporter-checkstyle` has been renamed to `ktlint-cli-reporter-checkstyle`. Class below has been relocated:
155→
156→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-reporter-checkstyle` | New class/package name in `ktlint-cli-reporter-checkstyle` |
157→|--------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------|
158→| com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.checkstyle               | com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.reporter.checkstyle               |
159→
160→Module `ktlint-reporter-format` has been renamed to `ktlint-cli-reporter-format`. Class below has been relocated:
161→
162→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-reporter-format` | New class/package name in `ktlint-cli-reporter-format` |
163→|----------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------|
164→| com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.format               | com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.reporter.format               |
165→
166→Module `ktlint-reporter-html` has been renamed to `ktlint-cli-reporter-html`. Class below has been relocated:
167→
168→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-reporter-html` | New class/package name in `ktlint-cli-reporter-html` |
169→|--------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------|
170→| com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.html               | com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.reporter.html               |
171→
172→Module `ktlint-reporter-json` has been renamed to `ktlint-cli-reporter-json`. Class below has been relocated:
173→
174→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-reporter-json` | New class/package name in `ktlint-cli-reporter-json` |
175→|--------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------|
176→| com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.json               | com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.reporter.json               |
177→
178→Module `ktlint-reporter-plain` has been renamed to `ktlint-cli-reporter-plain`. Class below has been relocated:
179→
180→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-reporter-plain` | New class/package name in `ktlint-cli-reporter-plain` |
181→|---------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------|
182→| com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.plain               | com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.reporter.plain               |
183→
184→Module `ktlint-reporter-plain-summary` has been renamed to `ktlint-cli-reporter-plain-summary`. Class below has been relocated:
185→
186→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-reporter-plain-summary` | New class/package name in `ktlint-cli-reporter-plain-summary` |
187→|-----------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------|
188→| com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.plain                       | com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.reporter.plainsummary                |
189→
190→Module `ktlint-reporter-sarif` has been renamed to `ktlint-cli-reporter-sarif`. Class below has been relocated:
191→
192→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-reporter-sarif` | New class/package name in `ktlint-cli-reporter-sarif` |
193→|---------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------|
194→| com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.sarif               | com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.reporter.sarif               |
195→
196→#### Custom Ruleset Provider `RuleSetProviderV2`
197→
198→Custom rule sets build for older versions of KtLint are no longer supported by this version of KtLint. The `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleSetProviderV2` interface has been replaced with `RuleSetProviderV3`. The accompanying interfaces `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleProvider` and `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Rule` have been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.core.api.RuleProvider` and `com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.core.api.Rule` respectively.
199→
200→Contrary to `RuleSetProviderV2`, the `RuleSetProviderV3` no longer contains information about the rule set. About information now has to be specified in the new `Rule` class. This allows custom rule set providers to combine rules originating from different rule sets into a new rule set without loosing information about its origin. The type of the id of the rule set is changed from `String` to `RuleSetId`.

content.thinking:
**Analyzing function expressions**

Okay, it seems like the expected behavior for closing quotes in function expressions should be at column 0. The way the rule processes extra indentation for function bodies might be wrong. They mentioned "Allow" in the comments, not "Require," which could potentially lead to conflicts. The bug I'm noticing is with an example where the closing quotes aren't aligning with the expected indentation. I think they might need to allow both 0 and 4 spaces for the closing quotes.**Refining function expression rules**

I think a potential fix for function expressions is to treat them like properties by using expectedIndent instead of node.indent. This would allow for 0 indentation while still accommodating special cases. However, that could cause issues with examples where closing quotes are indented 4 spaces. The logic needs to allow for that pattern too. If the closing quotes start with 4 spaces, our updated logic should recognize that and not flag it. So, 4 spaces seem to be the correct expected indent here.**Clarifying indentation for function signatures**

In the provided "Allow" example, it seems they want closing quotes to be indented 4 spaces rather than 0. This might be because the function signature spans multiple lines, which creates an indentation context that expects 4 spaces after the closing parenthesis. The closing quotes align with the indentation of the string, which could follow Kotlin formatting by IntelliJ. This makes sense since the opening quotes don't start at the line's beginning but align with the content instead. I'll keep this in mind when reviewing.**Examining closing quote indentation**

IntelliJ seems to expect closing quotes for function expressions to be indented 4 spaces, but for properties, it might expect 0 spaces. However, `val foo = """` could have closing quotes at 0 as well. The issue arises if they want properties to mimic function indentation. If so, that could contradict standard style unless their new rule mandates that closing quotes align one level more whenever opening quotes follow an "=" that isn't at the line's start. Currently, properties don't adhere to this, indicating a possible bug in the logic.**Understanding indentation context**

I’m trying to figure out how the startIndentContext works for EQ, particularly in relation to eqOrBlock and childIndent. It seems this context is pushed onto the stack after the outer function context. During visitFun, the order in which contexts are created might be affecting expected indentation. If the outer fun context is on top, it could shadow the inner ones. The outer context has an empty child indent, so I’m puzzled about how `correctedExpectedIndent` ends up being indented. It looks like for string templates, there’s this special case that changes the expected behavior for top-level functions, but not for properties?

content.thinking:
**Refining logic for indentation**

I'm thinking it might be better to unify the logic for determining correctedExpectedIndent by considering if the string template is on the same line as "=" and if "=" belongs to FUN or PROPERTY. 

If the property value starts and ends on different lines, there aren't violations. So, closing quotes should match the indentation of the property declaration line, which aligns with top-level properties being 0 and nested properties being 4.

**Analyzing code indentation issues**

I’m looking at the original code, and line 3096 has an indent of 0 with `$MULTILINE_STRING_QUOTE.trimIndent()` having an indent of 4. For `foo2` and `foo3`, there are lint violations at lines 10 and 15 regarding closing quotes. It seems that property raw string closing quotes can be misindented. While property assignments expect closing quotes aligned with property indent, function rules align them with content indentation. This inconsistency is puzzling!**Examining function and property indentations**

I'm noticing an issue where I want properties and functions to be treated similarly. Many tests rely on current property expectations, but some tests are showing function raw string results that expect closing quotes aligned with content indentation instead. This behavior seems to be adopted. So I wonder if my interpretation of the tests is off? In the function raw string result test at line 5062, closing quotes align with 4 indent — but are they indeed aligned with statement indent? It's worth double-checking!**Clarifying string indentation rules**

I’m analyzing the indentation of a specific line: the opening quotes are at indent 0 while the closing quotes line matches the indentation of the `.trimIndent()` call, which could signal a statement continuation. According to Kotlin's conventions, chaining calls can be indented by 4. When the closing quotes are on the same line as `.trimIndent()`, they align with its indentation rather than the content. For the top-level property, closing quotes are at indent 0, aligning with the `.trimIndent()` call, which is consistent.**Understanding closing quotes alignment**

I’m digging into how the closing quotes align with the `.trimIndent()` line, which has a statement indent of 0. For function expressions, the `.trimIndent()` line may be indented by 4 due to continuation indent after a line break. In the function raw string tests, `.trimIndent()` appears on the same line as closing quotes, and both are indented 4. This aligns with the rule that subsequent lines should be indented by 4 in multiline expressions after an `=`. It's starting to make more sense now!**Navigating property and function indents**

I'm contemplating whether properties and functions should share the same indentation rules. For the property example with `val foo = """`, the content is indented 4, while the closing quotes are at 0, raising questions about enforcement of continuation indents. The current behavior might be a bug since property indentation should ideally reflect function conventions. However, properties don't trigger the same indent context activation, as newlines inside strings don't affect expected indentation. This inconsistency could warrant a fix, but I must carefully consider the existing tests to avoid conflicts.**Refining indentation rules for functions and properties**

I'm contemplating whether we should verify indentation violations for closing quotes in properties and functions. It seems that while properties might not enforce a violation for closing quotes, we could change the function snippets to allow them at statement indent (0) as well. To align both properties and functions, we can adjust the logic in the StringTemplateIndenter. This will allow flexibility in indentation requirements, including handling returns in block bodies while maintaining consistency across tests. It's a complex process, but I'm confident we can streamline it!**Planning tests for indentation rules**

I'm considering how to handle actualIndent mismatches in autocorrection. It seems best to keep autocorrecting to correctedExpectedIndent for consistency across functions. Now, for testing, I’ll create a new test file called `IndentationRuleMultilineStringClosingQuotesTest.kt`. I plan to include at least two tests: one for function body expressions with closing quotes aligned at statement indent (0), and another for return statements with closing quotes aligned at return indent (4), both expecting no lint violations. I'll check existing tests to ensure consistency and gather the necessary helper functions too!

content.text:
Implemented a minimal fix in `IndentationRule` so multiline raw string closing quotes are handled consistently between functions and properties:

- In `StringTemplateIndenter.visitClosingQuotes`, when the raw string is the *result of a function* (either `fun foo() = """...` or `return """...`), the rule now accepts **either**:
  - closing quotes aligned with the function/return statement, **or**
  - closing quotes aligned one indent deeper (the existing accepted style)

Added a new test file `IndentationRuleMultilineStringClosingQuotesTest.kt` covering both repro cases (`fun foo() = ...` and `return ...`) ensuring they no longer emit `Unexpected indent of multiline string closing quotes`.

Relevant changes:
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/IndentationRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/test/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/IndentationRuleMultilineStringClosingQuotesTest.kt`

1→# Changelog
  2→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4→
  5→## Unreleased
  6→
  7→### Added
  8→
  9→### Removed
 10→
 11→### Fixed
 12→* Store path of file containing a lint violation relative to the location of the baseline file itself ([#1962](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1962))
 13→* Print absolute path of file in lint violations when flag "--relative" is not specified in Ktlint CLI ([#1963](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1963)) 
 14→* Handle parameter `--code-style=android_studio` in Ktlint CLI identical to deprecated parameter `--android` ([#1982](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1982))
 15→* Prevent nullpointer exception (NPE) if class without body is followed by multiple blank lines until end of file `no-consecutive-blank-lines` ([#1987](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1987))
 16→* Allow to 'unset' the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_function_signature_rule_force_multiline_when_parameter_count_greater_or_equal_than` when using `ktlint_official` code style `function-signature` ([#1977](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1977))
 17→* Prevent nullpointer exception (NPE) if or operator at start of line is followed by dot qualified expression `indent` ([#1993](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1993))
 18→
 19→### Changed
 20→* Separated Baseline functionality out of `ktlint-cli` into separate `ktlint-baseline` module for API consumers
 21→
 22→## [0.49.0] - 2023-04-21
 23→
 24→WARNING: This version of KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgraded before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
 25→
 26→All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
 27→* The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI.
 28→* The `.editorconfig` properties used to enable or disable rule and rule sets. Note that properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` have been removed in this release. See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.
 29→* The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
 30→* The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
 31→* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
 32→
 33→### Moving experimental rules to standard rule set
 34→
 35→The `experimental` rule set has been merged with `standard` rule set. The rules which formerly were part of the `experimental` rule set are still being treated as before. The rules will only be run in case `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or in case the rule is explicitly enabled.
 36→
 37→Note that the prefix `experimental:` has to be removed from all references to this rule. Check references in:
 38→* The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI.
 39→* The `.editorconfig` properties used to enable or disable rule and rule sets. Note that properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` have been removed in this release. See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.
 40→* The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
 41→* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
 42→
 43→### Promote experimental rules to non-experimental
 44→
 45→The rules below have been promoted to non-experimental rules:
 46→* [block-comment-initial-star-alignment](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#block-comment-initial-star-alignment)
 47→* [class-naming](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#classobject-naming)
 48→* [comment-wrapping](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#comment-wrapping)
 49→* [function-return-type-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#function-return-type-spacing)
 50→* [function-start-of-body-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#function-start-of-body-spacing)
 51→* [function-type-reference-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#function-type-reference-spacing)
 52→* [fun-keyword-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#fun-keyword-spacing)
 53→* [kdoc-wrapping](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#kdoc-wrapping)
 54→* [modifier-list-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#modifier-list-spacing)
 55→* [nullable-type-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#nullable-type-spacing)
 56→* [spacing-between-function-name-and-opening-parenthesis](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#spacing-between-function-name-and-opening-parenthesis)
 57→* [unnecessary-parentheses-before-trailing-lambda](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#unnecessary-parenthesis-before-trailing-lambda)
 58→
 59→Note that this only affects users that have enabled the standard ruleset while having the experimental rules disabled.
 60→
 61→### API Changes & RuleSet providers & Reporter Providers
 62→
 63→This release is intended to be the last release before the 1.0.x release of ktlint. If all goes as planned, the 1.0.x release does not contain any new breaking changes with except of removal of functionality which is deprecated in this release.
 64→
 65→This release contains a lot of breaking changes which aims to improve the future maintainability of Ktlint. If you get stuck while migrating, please reach out to us by creating an issue.
 66→
 67→#### Experimental rules
 68→
 69→Rules in custom rule sets can now be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint if `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or if the rule itself has been enabled explicitly.
 70→
 71→When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set as that would require a distinct rule set id. When moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set this would mean that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
 72→
 73→#### EditorConfig
 74→
 75→Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. New fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
 76→
 77→The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are no longer supported. Specifying those properties in the `editorConfigOverride` or `editorConfigDefaults` result in warnings at runtime.
 78→
 79→#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
 80→
 81→A new code style `ktlint_official` is introduced. This code style is work in progress but will become the default code style in the `1.0` release. Please try out the new code style and provide your feedback via the [issue tracker](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues).
 82→
 83→This `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This code style also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in those conventions and style guide. But do note that this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor.
 84→
 85→The existing code styles have been renamed to make more clear what the basis of the code style is.
 86→
 87→The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint `0.49`. Note that the default code style will be changed to `ktlint_official` in the `1.0` release.
 88→
 89→Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
 90→
 91→#### Package restructuring and class relocation
 92→
 93→The internal structure of the Ktlint project has been revised. The Ktlint CLI and KtLint API modules have been decoupled where possible. Modules have been restructured and renamed. See [API Overview](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/api/overview/) for more information.
 94→
 95→This is the last release that contains module `ktlint-core` as it had too many responsibilities. All classes in this module are relocated to other modules. Some classes have also been renamed. See tables below for details. Classes that are left behind in the `ktlint-core` module are deprecated and were kept in this version for backwards compatibility only. The `ktlint-core` module will be removed in Ktlint `0.50.x`.
 96→
 97→Classes below have been moved from module `ktlint-core` to the new module `ktlint-rule-engine-core`:
 98→
 99→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`                  | New class/package name in `ktlint-rule-engine-core`                               |
100→|----------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------|

196→#### Custom Ruleset Provider `RuleSetProviderV2`
197→
198→Custom rule sets build for older versions of KtLint are no longer supported by this version of KtLint. The `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleSetProviderV2` interface has been replaced with `RuleSetProviderV3`. The accompanying interfaces `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleProvider` and `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Rule` have been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.core.api.RuleProvider` and `com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.core.api.Rule` respectively.
199→
200→Contrary to `RuleSetProviderV2`, the `RuleSetProviderV3` no longer contains information about the rule set. About information now has to be specified in the new `Rule` class. This allows custom rule set providers to combine rules originating from different rule sets into a new rule set without loosing information about its origin. The type of the id of the rule set is changed from `String` to `RuleSetId`.

 126→public class IndentationRule :
 127→    StandardRule(
 128→        id = "indent",
 129→        visitorModifiers =
 130→            setOf(
 131→                VisitorModifier.RunAsLateAsPossible,
 132→                VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 133→                    ruleId = FUNCTION_SIGNATURE_RULE_ID,
 134→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 135→                ),
 136→                VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 137→                    ruleId = TRAILING_COMMA_ON_CALL_SITE_RULE_ID,
 138→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 139→                ),
 140→                VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 141→                    ruleId = TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_RULE_ID,
 142→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 143→                ),
 144→            ),
 145→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 146→            setOf(
 147→                CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 148→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 149→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 150→            ),
 151→    ) {
 152→    private var codeStyle = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 153→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 154→
 155→    private var line = 1
 156→
 157→    private val indentContextStack: Deque<IndentContext> = LinkedList()
 158→
 159→    private lateinit var stringTemplateIndenter: StringTemplateIndenter
 160→
 161→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 162→        codeStyle = editorConfig[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
 163→        indentConfig =
 164→            IndentConfig(
 165→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 166→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 167→            )
 168→        if (indentConfig.disabled) {
 169→            stopTraversalOfAST()
 170→        }
 171→    }
 172→
 173→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 174→        node: ASTNode,
 175→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 176→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 177→    ) {
 178→        if (node.isRoot()) {
 179→            // File should not start with a whitespace
 180→            node
 181→                .nextLeaf()
 182→                ?.takeIf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
 183→                ?.let { whitespaceWithoutNewline ->
 184→                    emit(node.startOffset, "Unexpected indentation", true)
 185→                    if (autoCorrect) {
 186→                        whitespaceWithoutNewline.treeParent.removeChild(whitespaceWithoutNewline)
 187→                    }
 188→                }
 189→            indentContextStack.addLast(startNoIndentZone(node))
 190→        }
 191→
 192→        when {
 193→            node.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() -> {
 194→                line++
 195→                if (indentContextStack.peekLast()?.activated == false) {
 196→                    val lastIndentContext = indentContextStack.removeLast()
 197→                    indentContextStack.addLast(
 198→                        lastIndentContext.copy(activated = true),
 199→                    )
 200→                }
 201→                visitNewLineIndentation(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 202→            }
 203→
 204→            node.elementType == CLASS_BODY ||
 205→                node.elementType == CONTEXT_RECEIVER_LIST ||
 206→                node.elementType == LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY ||
 207→                node.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_CALL_ENTRY ||
 208→                node.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE ||
 209→                node.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST ->
 210→                startIndentContext(
 211→                    fromAstNode = node,
 212→                    lastChildIndent = "",
 213→                )
 214→
 215→            node.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT ->
 216→                visitValueArgument(node)
 217→
 218→            node.elementType == SECONDARY_CONSTRUCTOR ->
 219→                visitSecondaryConstructor(node)
 220→
 221→            node.elementType == PARENTHESIZED &&
 222→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != IF ->
 223→                startIndentContext(node)
 224→
 225→            node.elementType == BINARY_WITH_TYPE ||
 226→                node.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY ||
 227→                node.elementType == TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST ||
 228→                node.elementType == TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST ||
 229→                node.elementType == USER_TYPE ->
 230→                startIndentContext(node)
 231→
 232→            node.elementType == DELEGATED_SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY ||
 233→                node.elementType == ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION ||
 234→                node.elementType == TYPE_REFERENCE ->
 235→                startIndentContext(
 236→                    fromAstNode = node,
 237→                    childIndent = "",
 238→                )
 239→
 240→            node.elementType == IF ->
 241→                visitIf(node)
 242→
 243→            node.elementType == LBRACE ->
 244→                visitLbrace(node)
 245→
 246→            node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST &&
 247→                node.treeParent.elementType != FUNCTION_LITERAL ->
 248→                startIndentContext(
 249→                    fromAstNode = node,
 250→                    lastChildIndent = "",
 251→                )
 252→
 253→            node.elementType == LPAR &&
 254→                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == CONDITION ->
 255→                visitLparBeforeCondition(node)
 256→
 257→            node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER ->
 258→                visitValueParameter(node)
 259→
 260→            node.elementType == FUN ->
 261→                visitFun(node)
 262→
 263→            node.elementType == CLASS ->
 264→                visitClass(node)
 265→
 266→            node.elementType == OBJECT_DECLARATION ->
 267→                visitObjectDeclaration(node)
 268→
 269→            node.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION ->
 270→                visitBinaryExpression(node)
 271→
 272→            node.elementType in CHAINABLE_EXPRESSION -> {
 273→                if (codeStyle == ktlint_official &&
 274→                    node.elementType == DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION &&
 275→                    node.treeParent?.elementType == ARRAY_ACCESS_EXPRESSION &&
 276→                    node.treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION
 277→                ) {
 278→                    // Issue 1540: Deviate and fix from incorrect formatting in IntelliJ IDEA formatting and produce following:
 279→                    // val fooBar2 = foo
 280→                    //    .bar[0] {
 281→                    //        "foobar"
 282→                    //    }
 283→                    startIndentContext(
 284→                        fromAstNode = node.treeParent,
 285→                        toAstNode = node.treeParent.treeParent.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 286→                    )
 287→                } else if (node.prevCodeSibling().isElvisOperator()) {
 288→                    startIndentContext(node)
 289→                } else if (node.treeParent.elementType in CHAINABLE_EXPRESSION) {
 290→                    // Multiple dot qualified expressions and/or safe expression on the same line should not increase the indent level
 291→                } else {
 292→                    startIndentContext(node)
 293→                }
 294→            }
 295→
 296→            node.elementType == IDENTIFIER &&
 297→                node.treeParent.elementType == PROPERTY ->
 298→                visitIdentifierInProperty(node)
 299→
 300→            node.elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY &&
 301→                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE ->
 302→                visitWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingQuote(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 303→
 304→            node.elementType == WHEN ->
 305→                visitWhen(node)
 306→
 307→            node.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY ->
 308→                visitWhenEntry(node)
 309→
 310→            node.elementType == WHERE_KEYWORD &&
 311→                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST ->
 312→                visitWhereKeywordBeforeTypeConstraintList(node)
 313→
 314→            node.elementType == KDOC ->
 315→                visitKdoc(node)
 316→
 317→            node.elementType == PROPERTY_ACCESSOR ||
 318→                node.elementType == TYPEALIAS ->
 319→                visitPropertyAccessor(node)
 320→
 321→            node.elementType == FOR ||
 322→                node.elementType == WHILE ->
 323→                visitConditionalLoop(node)
 324→
 325→            node.elementType == LBRACKET ->
 326→                visitLBracket(node)
 327→
 328→            node.elementType == NULLABLE_TYPE ->
 329→                visitNullableType(node)
 330→
 331→            node.elementType == DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION ->
 332→                visitDestructuringDeclaration(node)
 333→
 334→            node.elementType == TRY ->
 335→                visitTryCatchFinally(node)
 336→
 337→            else -> {
 338→                LOGGER.trace { "No processing for ${node.elementType}: ${node.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()}" }
 339→            }
 340→        }
 341→    }
 342→
 343→    private fun visitValueArgument(node: ASTNode) {
 344→        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official) {
 345→            // Deviate from standard IntelliJ IDEA formatting to allow formatting below:
 346→            //     val foo = foo(
 347→            //         parameterName =
 348→            //             "The quick brown fox "
 349→            //                .plus("jumps ")
 350→            //                .plus("over the lazy dog"),
 351→            //     )
 352→            startIndentContext(
 353→                fromAstNode = node,
 354→                lastChildIndent = "",
 355→            )
 356→        }
 357→    }
 358→
 359→    private fun visitSecondaryConstructor(node: ASTNode) {
 360→        node
 361→            .findChildByType(CONSTRUCTOR_DELEGATION_CALL)
 362→            ?.let { constructorDelegationCall ->
 363→                val fromAstNode = node.skipLeadingWhitespaceCommentsAndAnnotations()
 364→                val nextToAstNode =
 365→                    startIndentContext(
 366→                        fromAstNode = constructorDelegationCall,
 367→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 368→
 369→                // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as constructor itself
 370→                if (fromAstNode != node.nextLeaf()) {
 371→                    startIndentContext(
 372→                        fromAstNode = node,
 373→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 374→                        childIndent = "",
 375→                    )
 376→                }
 377→            }
 378→    }
 379→
 380→    private fun visitIf(node: ASTNode) {
 381→        var nextToAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 382→        node
 383→            .findChildByType(ELSE)
 384→            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 385→                nextToAstNode =
 386→                    startIndentContext(
 387→                        fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 388→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 389→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 390→            }
 391→
 392→        node
 393→            .findChildByType(THEN)
 394→            ?.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 395→            ?.nextLeaf()
 396→            ?.let { nodeAfterThenBlock ->
 397→                nextToAstNode =
 398→                    startIndentContext(
 399→                        fromAstNode = nodeAfterThenBlock,
 400→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 401→                        childIndent = "",
 402→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 403→            }
 404→        node
 405→            .findChildByType(RPAR)
 406→            ?.nextCodeLeaf()
 407→            ?.let { nodeAfterConditionBlock ->
 408→                nextToAstNode =
 409→                    startIndentContext(
 410→                        fromAstNode = nodeAfterConditionBlock,
 411→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 412→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 413→            }
 414→        startIndentContext(
 415→            fromAstNode = node,
 416→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 417→            lastChildIndent = "", // No indent for the RPAR
 418→        )
 419→    }
 420→
 421→    private fun visitLbrace(node: ASTNode) {
 422→        // Outer indent context
 423→        val rbrace =
 424→            requireNotNull(
 425→                node.nextSibling { it.elementType == RBRACE },
 426→            ) { "Can not find matching rbrace" }
 427→        startIndentContext(
 428→            fromAstNode = node,
 429→            toAstNode = rbrace,
 430→            firstChildIndent = "",
 431→            lastChildIndent = "",
 432→        )
 433→
 434→        // Inner indent context in reversed order
 435→        node
 436→            .treeParent
 437→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == FUNCTION_LITERAL }
 438→            ?.findChildByType(ARROW)
 439→            ?.let { arrow ->
 440→                startIndentContext(
 441→                    fromAstNode = arrow,
 442→                    toAstNode = rbrace,
 443→                    lastChildIndent = "",
 444→                )
 445→                startIndentContext(
 446→                    fromAstNode = node,
 447→                    toAstNode = arrow.prevCodeLeaf()!!,
 448→                    childIndent = arrow.calculateIndentOfFunctionLiteralParameters(),
 449→                )
 450→            }
 451→    }
 452→
 453→    private fun ASTNode.calculateIndentOfFunctionLiteralParameters() =
 454→        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official || isFirstParameterOfFunctionLiteralPrecededByNewLine()) {
 455→            // val fieldExample =
 456→            //      LongNameClass {
 457→            //              paramA,
 458→            //              paramB,
 459→            //              paramC ->
 460→            //          ClassB(paramA, paramB, paramC)
 461→            //      }
 462→            indentConfig.indent.repeat(2)
 463→        } else {
 464→            // Allow default IntelliJ IDEA formatting:
 465→            // val fieldExample =
 466→            //     LongNameClass { paramA,
 467→            //                     paramB,
 468→            //                     paramC ->
 469→            //         ClassB(paramA, paramB, paramC)
 470→            //     }
 471→            parent(CALL_EXPRESSION)
 472→                ?.let { callExpression ->
 473→                    val textBeforeFirstParameter =
 474→                        callExpression.findChildByType(REFERENCE_EXPRESSION)?.text +
 475→                            " { "
 476→                    " ".repeat(textBeforeFirstParameter.length)
 477→                }
 478→                ?: indentConfig.indent.repeat(2)
 479→        }
 480→
 481→    private fun ASTNode.isFirstParameterOfFunctionLiteralPrecededByNewLine() =
 482→        parent(FUNCTION_LITERAL)
 483→            ?.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
 484→            ?.prevSibling { it.textContains('\n') } != null
 485→
 486→    private fun visitLparBeforeCondition(node: ASTNode) {
 487→        startIndentContext(
 488→            fromAstNode = requireNotNull(node.nextLeaf()), // Allow to pickup whitespace before condition
 489→            toAstNode = requireNotNull(node.nextCodeSibling()).lastChildLeafOrSelf(), // Ignore whitespace after condition but before rpar
 490→            nodeIndent = currentIndent() + indentConfig.indent,
 491→            childIndent = "",
 492→        )
 493→    }
 494→
 495→    private fun visitValueParameter(node: ASTNode) {
 496→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 497→        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 498→        node
 499→            .findChildByType(EQ)
 500→            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 501→                nextToAstNode =
 502→                    startIndentContext(
 503→                        fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 504→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 505→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 506→            }
 507→
 508→        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official) {
 509→            // Deviate from standard IntelliJ IDEA formatting to allow formatting below:
 510→            //     fun process(
 511→            //         aVariableWithAVeryLongName:
 512→            //             TypeWithAVeryLongNameThatDoesNotFitOnSameLineAsTheVariableName
 513→            //     ): List<Output>
 514→            node
 515→                .findChildByType(COLON)
 516→                ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 517→                    nextToAstNode =
 518→                        startIndentContext(
 519→                            fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 520→                            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 521→                        ).prevCodeLeaf()
 522→                }
 523→        }
 524→
 525→        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as constructor itself
 526→        val fromAstNode = node.skipLeadingWhitespaceCommentsAndAnnotations()
 527→        if (fromAstNode != node.firstChildNode &&
 528→            node.prevSibling { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() } == null &&
 529→            node == node.treeParent.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER)
 530→        ) {
 531→            nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 532→                fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 533→                toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 534→            ).fromASTNode.prevLeaf { !it.isWhiteSpace() }!!
 535→        } else {
 536→            startIndentContext(
 537→                fromAstNode = node,
 538→                toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 539→                childIndent = "",
 540→            )
 541→        }
 542→    }
 543→
 544→    private fun visitFun(node: ASTNode) {
 545→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 546→        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 547→        val eqOrBlock =
 548→            node.findChildByType(EQ)
 549→                ?: node.findChildByType(BLOCK)
 550→        eqOrBlock?.let {
 551→            nextToAstNode =
 552→                startIndentContext(
 553→                    fromAstNode = eqOrBlock,
 554→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 555→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 556→        }
 557→
 558→        node
 559→            .findChildByType(TYPE_REFERENCE)
 560→            ?.let { typeReference ->
 561→                nextToAstNode =
 562→                    startIndentContext(
 563→                        fromAstNode = typeReference.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 564→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 565→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 566→            }
 567→
 568→        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as function itself
 569→        startIndentContext(
 570→            fromAstNode = node,
 571→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 572→            childIndent = "",
 573→        )
 574→    }
 575→
 576→    private fun visitClass(node: ASTNode) {
 577→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 578→        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 579→        node
 580→            .findChildByType(WHERE_KEYWORD)
 581→            ?.let { where ->
 582→                val typeConstraintList =
 583→                    requireNotNull(
 584→                        where.nextCodeSibling(),
 585→                    ) { "Can not find code sibling after WHERE in CLASS" }
 586→                require(
 587→                    typeConstraintList.elementType == TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST,
 588→                ) { "Code sibling after WHERE in CLASS is not a TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST" }
 589→                nextToAstNode =
 590→                    startIndentContext(
 591→                        fromAstNode = where.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 592→                        toAstNode = typeConstraintList.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 593→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 594→            }
 595→
 596→        val primaryConstructor = node.findChildByType(PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR)
 597→        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official && primaryConstructor != null) {
 598→            // Indent both constructor and super type list
 599→            nextToAstNode =
 600→                startIndentContext(
 601→                    fromAstNode = primaryConstructor.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 602→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 603→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 604→        } else {
 605→            node
 606→                .findChildByType(SUPER_TYPE_LIST)
 607→                ?.let { superTypeList ->
 608→                    nextToAstNode =
 609→                        startIndentContext(
 610→                            fromAstNode = superTypeList.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 611→                            toAstNode = superTypeList.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 612→                        ).prevCodeLeaf()
 613→                }
 614→        }
 615→
 616→        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as class itself
 617→        startIndentContext(
 618→            fromAstNode = node,
 619→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 620→            childIndent = "",
 621→        )
 622→    }

 624→    private fun visitObjectDeclaration(node: ASTNode) {
 625→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 626→        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 627→        node
 628→            .findChildByType(SUPER_TYPE_LIST)
 629→            ?.let { superTypeList ->
 630→                nextToAstNode =
 631→                    startIndentContext(
 632→                        fromAstNode = superTypeList.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 633→                        toAstNode = superTypeList.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 634→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 635→            }
 636→
 637→        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as class itself
 638→        startIndentContext(
 639→            fromAstNode = node,
 640→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 641→            childIndent = "",
 642→        )
 643→    }

 645→    private fun visitBinaryExpression(node: ASTNode) {
 646→        if (isPartOfBinaryExpressionWrappedInCondition(node)) {
 647→            // Complex binary expression are nested in such a way that the indent context of the condition
 648→            // wrapper is not the last node on the stack
 649→            val conditionIndentContext =
 650→                indentContextStack.last { it.fromASTNode.elementType != BINARY_EXPRESSION }
 651→            // Create new indent context for the remainder (operator and right-hand side) of the binary expression
 652→            startIndentContext(
 653→                fromAstNode = node.findChildByType(OPERATION_REFERENCE)!!,
 654→                toAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 655→                nodeIndent = conditionIndentContext.nodeIndent,
 656→                childIndent = conditionIndentContext.childIndent,
 657→            )
 658→            startIndentContext(node.firstChildNode)
 659→        } else if (node.treeParent?.elementType != BINARY_EXPRESSION ||
 660→            node.findChildByType(OPERATION_REFERENCE)?.firstChildNode?.elementType == ELVIS
 661→        ) {
 662→            startIndentContext(node)
 663→        }
 664→    }

 666→    private fun visitIdentifierInProperty(node: ASTNode) {
 667→        startIndentContext(
 668→            fromAstNode = node,
 669→            toAstNode = node.treeParent.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 670→        )
 671→    }

 673→    private fun visitWhen(node: ASTNode) {
 674→        var nextToASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 675→        node
 676→            .findChildByType(LPAR)
 677→            ?.let { lpar ->
 678→                nextToASTNode =
 679→                    startIndentContext(
 680→                        fromAstNode = lpar,
 681→                        toAstNode = node.findChildByType(RPAR)!!,
 682→                        lastChildIndent = "",
 683→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 684→            }
 685→        startIndentContext(
 686→            fromAstNode = node,
 687→            toAstNode = nextToASTNode,
 688→        )
 689→    }
 690→
 691→    private fun visitWhenEntry(node: ASTNode) {
 692→        node
 693→            .findChildByType(ARROW)
 694→            ?.let { arrow ->
 695→                val outerIndent =
 696→                    if (arrow.nextLeaf()?.elementType == BLOCK) {
 697→                        ""
 698→                    } else {
 699→                        indentConfig.indent
 700→                    }
 701→                startIndentContext(
 702→                    fromAstNode = arrow.nextLeaf()!!,
 703→                    toAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 704→                    firstChildIndent = outerIndent,
 705→                    lastChildIndent = outerIndent,
 706→                )
 707→                startIndentContext(
 708→                    fromAstNode = node,
 709→                    toAstNode = arrow,
 710→                    childIndent = "",
 711→                )
 712→            }
 713→    }

 715→    private fun visitWhereKeywordBeforeTypeConstraintList(node: ASTNode) {
 716→        startIndentContext(
 717→            fromAstNode = node,
 718→            toAstNode = node.nextCodeSibling()?.lastChildLeafOrSelf()!!,
 719→            childIndent = TYPE_CONSTRAINT_CONTINUATION_INDENT,
 720→        )
 721→    }
 722→
 723→    private fun visitKdoc(node: ASTNode) {
 724→        node
 725→            .findChildByType(KDOC_START)
 726→            ?.nextLeaf()
 727→            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 728→                startIndentContext(
 729→                    fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 730→                    toAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 731→                    childIndent = KDOC_CONTINUATION_INDENT,
 732→                )
 733→            }

 736→    private fun visitPropertyAccessor(node: ASTNode) {
 737→        var nextToASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 738→        node
 739→            .findChildByType(EQ)
 740→            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 741→                nextToASTNode =
 742→                    startIndentContext(
 743→                        fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 744→                        toAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 745→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 746→            }
 747→        // No indent on preceding annotations and comments
 748→        startIndentContext(
 749→            fromAstNode = node,

 752→        )

 755→    private fun visitConditionalLoop(node: ASTNode) {
 756→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 757→        node
 758→            .findChildByType(BODY)
 759→            ?.takeIf { it.nextCodeLeaf()?.elementType != LBRACE }
 760→            ?.let { rpar ->
 761→                startIndentContext(
 762→                    fromAstNode = rpar,
 763→                    toAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 764→                )
 765→            }
 766→        node
 767→            .findChildByType(CONDITION)
 768→            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 769→                startIndentContext(fromAstNode)
 770→            }
 771→    }
 772→
 773→    private fun visitLBracket(node: ASTNode) {
 774→        node
 775→            .treeParent
 776→            .takeUnless {
 777→                // Should be resolved in IntelliJ IDEA default formatter:
 778→                // https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KTIJ-14859/Too-little-indentation-inside-the-brackets-in-multiple-annotations-with-the-same-target
 779→                it.elementType == ANNOTATION
 780→            }?.findChildByType(RBRACKET)
 781→            ?.let { rbracket ->
 782→                startIndentContext(
 783→                    fromAstNode = node,
 784→                    toAstNode = rbracket,
 785→                    firstChildIndent = "",
 786→                    lastChildIndent = "",
 787→                )
 788→            }
 789→    }

 791→    private fun visitNullableType(node: ASTNode) {

 796→            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 797→                nextToAstNode =
 798→                    startIndentContext(
 799→                        fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 800→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,

 810→    private fun visitDestructuringDeclaration(node: ASTNode) {
 811→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 812→        var nextToAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 813→        node
 814→            .findChildByType(EQ)
 815→            ?.let { eq ->
 816→                nextToAstNode =
 817→                    startIndentContext(
 818→                        fromAstNode = eq,
 819→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 820→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 821→            }
 822→
 823→        node
 824→            .findChildByType(RPAR)
 825→            ?.let {
 826→                startIndentContext(

 828→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,

 834→    private fun visitTryCatchFinally(node: ASTNode) {
 835→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 836→        var nextToAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 837→        node
 838→            .findChildByType(FINALLY)
 839→            ?.let { finally ->
 840→                nextToAstNode =
 841→                    startIndentContext(
 842→                        fromAstNode = finally,
 843→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 844→                        childIndent = "",
 845→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 846→            }
 847→
 848→        node
 849→            .findChildByType(CATCH)
 850→            ?.let { catch ->
 851→                nextToAstNode =
 852→                    startIndentContext(
 853→                        fromAstNode = catch,
 854→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 855→                        childIndent = "",
 856→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 857→            }
 858→
 859→        startIndentContext(
 860→            fromAstNode = node,
 861→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 862→            lastChildIndent = "",
 863→        )
 864→    }

 866→    private fun ASTNode.skipLeadingWhitespaceCommentsAndAnnotations(): ASTNode {
 867→        require(elementType == SECONDARY_CONSTRUCTOR || elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER)
 868→        return findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
 869→            ?.let { modifierList ->
 870→                modifierList
 871→                    .children()
 872→                    .firstOrNull { !it.isWhiteSpace() && !it.isPartOfComment() && it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
 873→                    ?: modifierList.nextCodeSibling()
 874→            }
 875→            ?: children().firstOrNull { !it.isWhiteSpace() && !it.isPartOfComment() }
 876→            ?: this
 877→    }
 878→
 879→    private fun ASTNode.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(): ASTNode {
 880→        var fromAstNode: ASTNode? = this
 881→        while (fromAstNode?.prevLeaf() != null &&
 882→            (fromAstNode.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpace() || fromAstNode.prevLeaf()?.isPartOfComment() == true)
 883→        ) {
 884→            fromAstNode = fromAstNode.prevLeaf()
 885→        }
 886→        return fromAstNode!!
 887→    }
 888→
 889→    private fun isPartOfBinaryExpressionWrappedInCondition(node: ASTNode) =
 890→        node
 891→            .parents()
 892→            .takeWhile { it.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION || it.elementType == CONDITION }
 893→            .lastOrNull()
 894→            ?.elementType == CONDITION
 895→
 896→    private fun currentIndent() =
 897→        indentContextStack
 898→            .peekLast()
 899→            .indent()
 900→
 901→    private fun startIndentContext(
 902→        fromAstNode: ASTNode,
 903→        toAstNode: ASTNode = fromAstNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 904→        nodeIndent: String = currentIndent(),
 905→        childIndent: String = indentConfig.indent,
 906→        firstChildIndent: String = childIndent,
 907→        lastChildIndent: String = childIndent,
 908→    ): IndentContext =
 909→        IndentContext(
 910→            fromASTNode = fromAstNode,
 911→            toASTNode = toAstNode,
 912→            nodeIndent = nodeIndent,
 913→            firstChildIndent = firstChildIndent,
 914→            childIndent = childIndent,
 915→            lastChildIndent = lastChildIndent,
 916→        ).also { newIndentContext ->
 917→            LOGGER.trace {
 918→                val nodeIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(newIndentContext.nodeIndent)
 919→                val childIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(newIndentContext.childIndent)
 920→                "Create new indent context (same as parent) with level ($nodeIndentLevel, $childIndentLevel) for " +
 921→                    "${fromAstNode.elementType}: ${newIndentContext.nodes}"
 922→            }
 923→            indentContextStack.addLast(newIndentContext)
 924→        }
 925→
 926→    override fun afterVisitChildNodes(
 927→        node: ASTNode,
 928→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 929→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 930→    ) {
 931→        while (indentContextStack.peekLast()?.toASTNode == node) {
 932→            LOGGER.trace {
 933→                val indentContext = indentContextStack.peekLast()
 934→                val nodeIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(indentContext.nodeIndent)
 935→                val childIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(indentContext.childIndent)
 936→                "Remove indent context with level ($nodeIndentLevel, $childIndentLevel) for ${indentContext.fromASTNode.elementType}: " +
 937→                    "${indentContext.nodes}"
 938→            }
 939→            indentContextStack
 940→                .removeLast()
 941→                .also {
 942→                    LOGGER.trace {
 943→                        val indentContext = indentContextStack.peekLast()
 944→                        val nodeIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(indentContext.nodeIndent)

 947→                            "${indentContext.fromASTNode.elementType}: ${indentContext.nodes}"

 960→    private fun visitNewLineIndentation(
 961→        node: ASTNode,
 962→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 963→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 964→    ) {
 965→        if (node.ignoreIndent()) {
 966→            return
 967→        }
 968→
 969→        val normalizedNodeIndent = node.normalizedIndent(emit)
 970→        val expectedIndentation = node.expectedIndent()
 971→        val text = node.text
 972→        val nodeIndent = text.substringAfterLast("\n")
 973→        if (nodeIndent != normalizedNodeIndent || normalizedNodeIndent != expectedIndentation) {
 974→            if (normalizedNodeIndent != expectedIndentation) {
 975→                emit(
 976→                    node.startOffset + text.length - nodeIndent.length,
 977→                    "Unexpected indentation (${normalizedNodeIndent.length}) (should be ${expectedIndentation.length})",
 978→                    true,
 979→                )
 980→            } else {
 981→                // Indentation was at correct level but contained invalid indent characters. This violation has already
 982→                // been emitted.
 983→            }
 984→            LOGGER.trace {
 985→                "Line $line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "changed indentation to ${expectedIndentation.length} " +
 986→                    "(from ${normalizedNodeIndent.length}) for ${node.elementType}: ${node.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()}"
 987→            }
 988→            if (autoCorrect) {
 989→                (node as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(
 990→                    text.substringBeforeLast("\n") + "\n" + expectedIndentation,
 991→                )
 992→            }
 993→        } else {
 994→            node.processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
 995→        }
 996→    }

 998→    private fun ASTNode.ignoreIndent(): Boolean {
 999→        val nextLeaf = nextLeaf()
1000→        if (text.endsWith("\n") && nextLeaf.isStartOfRawStringLiteral()) {
1001→            processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
1002→            return true // raw strings (""") are allowed at column 0
1003→        }
1004→
1005→        nextLeaf
1006→            ?.parent(strict = false) { it.psi is PsiComment }
1007→            ?.let { comment ->
1008→                if (text.endsWith("\n")) {
1009→                    processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
1010→                    return true // comments are allowed at column 0
1011→                }
1012→                if (comment.textContains('\n') && comment.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT) {
1013→                    // FIXME: while we cannot assume any kind of layout inside a block comment,
1014→                    // `/*` and `*/` can still be indented
1015→                    processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
1016→                    return true
1017→                }
1018→            }
1019→        return false

1022→    private fun ASTNode?.isStartOfRawStringLiteral(): Boolean = this != null && this.elementType == OPEN_QUOTE && this.text == "\"\"\""

1024→    private fun ASTNode.processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded() =

1027→    private fun ASTNode.expectedIndent(): String {
1028→        val lastIndexContext = indentContextStack.peekLast()
1029→        val nextLeaf = nextLeaf()
1030→        val adjustedChildIndent =
1031→            when {
1032→                this == lastIndexContext.fromASTNode.firstChildLeafOrSelf() ||
1033→                    nextLeaf == lastIndexContext.fromASTNode.firstChildLeafOrSelf() ->
1034→                    lastIndexContext.firstChildIndent
1035→
1036→                this == lastIndexContext.toASTNode ||
1037→                    nextLeaf == lastIndexContext.toASTNode ->
1038→                    lastIndexContext.lastChildIndent
1039→
1040→                else -> lastIndexContext.childIndent
1041→            }
1042→        return lastIndexContext.nodeIndent + adjustedChildIndent
1043→    }
